# X Post Drafts

## Launch / participation posts

1. Building for the #NansenCLI mini hackathon with a reusable foundation called Nansen Query Planner. Week 1 shell: Thesis Battlefield — paste a token thesis, let the agent gather evidence, then see whether onchain reality supports or contradicts the claim. @nansen_ai #NansenCLI

2. I’m treating the #NansenCLI mini hackathon as one compounding build, not 4 disconnected projects. Core engine: Nansen Query Planner. Week 1 app: Thesis Battlefield. Input a thesis, output an evidence-backed verdict with visible query trace. @nansen_ai #NansenCLI

3. Working on Thesis Battlefield for the #NansenCLI challenge: bull agent, bear agent, and a judge agent all grounded by a cost-aware Nansen Query Planner. The goal is not another dashboard — it’s an agent that turns onchain data into a verdict. @nansen_ai #NansenCLI

4. Week 1 idea for #NansenCLI: Thesis Battlefield. Paste a market thesis, token claim, or tweet narrative, then let an agent use Nansen data to test it. Strongest bull case, strongest bear case, contradictions, confidence, and full query trace. @nansen_ai #NansenCLI

5. The interesting part of this #NansenCLI build isn’t just querying Nansen. It’s deciding which queries to run, in what order, under budget, to answer one concrete question well. That’s the role of Nansen Query Planner. @nansen_ai #NansenCLI
